U
Oracle Events
OE 1: List all engineering tickets for the Expense Auto-Categorization Engine project using linear_list_issues (project: "Expense Auto-Categorization Engine" or similar query). Expected findings: current state of all remediation tickets - ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, Lena, in_progress), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, Lena, in_progress), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, Lena, in_progress), ENG-218 (dashboard type validation, Anh, in_progress), ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, Samira, todo), ENG-220 (duplicate detection, Dmitri, todo), ENG-212 (edge case coverage, Dmitri, in_progress), ENG-216 (audit, Dmitri, in_progress), ENG-211 (pilot monitoring, Samira, in_progress).
OE 2: Read comments on key remediation tickets using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_pipeline_hardening" or "issue_launch_readiness_checklist" or similar) to find resolution details, status updates, or blockers posted by Lena, Anh, or Samira since mid-March.
OE 3: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for Maria Santos using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Maria Santos"). Expected finding: recStip000000018, $550 standing desk, Approval Status: "Rejected", still showing Pending manual correction in Review Notes.
OE 4: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for James Okafor using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Employee Name", value: "James Okafor"). Expected finding: recStip000000019, $82 keyboard tray, Approval Status: "Rejected", still showing Pending manual correction in Review Notes.
OE 5: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for Lisa Park using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Lisa Park"). Expected finding: recStip000000020, $130 WiFi system, Approval Status: "Rejected", still showing Pending manual correction in Review Notes.
OE 6: Search emails for receipt verification responses using search_emails (query: "receipt verification" or "TechComfort" or "Kevin Tran receipt" or "duplicate receipt" or similar). Expected findings: Dmitri's original escalation email to Marcus (email_dmitri_receipt_audit_findings), Marcus's reply confirming holds (email_marcus_qb_status_reply), Marcus's verification request to Mina for Kevin Tran (email_marcus_to_mina_receipt_hold), Marcus's verification request to Emeka for Andre Washington and Jordan Ekwueme (email_marcus_to_emeka_receipt_holds). Critically: NO verification response emails from Rachel Whitfield (Vectral), Tamara Byrd (Mosaic), or Tessa Moreno (BrightLoop) confirming or denying the receipts' legitimacy.
OE 7: Search emails for any follow-up from Mina or Emeka about the receipt verifications using search_emails (query: "Andre Washington" or "Jordan Ekwueme" or "Tamara Byrd" or "Tessa Moreno" or similar). Expected finding: Marcus's outbound requests exist but no inbound replies from client HR contacts confirming or denying the purchases.
OE 8: Search Slack for any updates about receipt verification or duplicate receipts using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "duplicate receipt" or "TechComfort" or "receipt verification" or "receipt hash" or similar). Expected findings: Dmitri's own Slack posts in #engineering announcing the findings and filing ENG-220, Samira's approval of the approach. No Slack messages from Mina or Emeka confirming that client HR contacts responded.
OE 9: Look up Samira Tariq's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira Tariq" or "Samira" or similar). Expected finding: samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE 10: Look up Marcus Thorne's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne" or "Marcus" or similar). Expected finding: marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
OE 11: Look up Dmitri Volkov's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Dmitri Volkov" or "Dmitri" or similar). Expected finding: dmitri.volkov@moveops.com (needed as sender for the email).
OE 12: Send email (via send_email) from dmitri.volkov@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com, with a structured relaunch readiness assessment. Subject relates to "ExpenseBot Relaunch Readiness Assessment" (or similar). Body must include: (a) status of each remediation ticket (ENG-214, 215, 217, 218, 219, 220, 212), (b) whether the three Vectral stipend corrections (Santos, Okafor, Park) have been completed, (c) status of the duplicate receipt verifications (Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme), (d) a go/no-go recommendation with reasoning.
OE 13: Add a comment on the duplicate detection ticket (via linear_create_comment) with issueId: "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection" and body documenting the current status of all three receipt verifications - whether Rachel Whitfield (Vectral), Tamara Byrd (Mosaic), and Tessa Moreno (BrightLoop) responded, and what the financial exposure status is ($89 paid for Kevin Tran, $89 and $45 on hold for Andre Washington and Jordan Ekwueme).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

18 The final response identifies that no verification responses have been received from client HR contacts regarding the three flagged duplicate receipts - Kevin Tran ($89, Vectral), Andre Washington ($89, Mosaic), and Jordan Ekwueme ($45, BrightLoop) - despite Marcus sending verification requests to Mina and Emeka on March 15, 2026.

 ✗✗—✗✗✓ 17%
✗Run #1The criterion requires identifying that NO verification responses were received from any of the three client HR contacts. However, the agent fabricated a finding that Kevin Tran's receipt was verified as legitimate by Rachel Whitfield. According to the ground truth, no reply emails exist from Rachel Whitfield, Tamara Byrd, or Tessa Moreno. The agent's final response incorrectly reports Kevin Tran as '✅ Verified legitimate' with a fabricated Slack reference, failing to correctly identify the complete absence of responses for all three receipts.
✗Run #2The criterion requires identifying that NO verification responses were received for all three receipts (Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme). The criterion annotation explicitly states 'no reply emails exist in the universe from Rachel Whitfield, Tamara Byrd, or Tessa Moreno.' However, the agent's final response states 'Kevin Tran (Vectral, $89): Rachel Whitfield confirmed to Mina it was legitimate' — fabricating/hallucinating a verification response. The agent failed to correctly identify the absence of verification for Kevin Tran's receipt.
—Run #3The agent correctly identified that Andre Washington and Jordan Ekwueme have no HR responses. However, for Kevin Tran it found a confirmation from Rachel Whitfield (which is consistent with no missing response for Kevin). The agent did not explicitly confirm 'no response received' for Kevin specifically — it found a confirmation email. The overall finding about two of three having no responses was correctly identified, though the agent presented Kevin as 'resolved' which technically means only 2 of 3 had no responses. The criterion asks for the finding to cover all three as having no responses; Kevin's appears as resolved, which may reflect actual data in the test universe rather than a failure.
✗Run #4The criterion expects the final response to identify that NO verification responses were received for all three receipts. However, the final response explicitly states 'Kevin Tran ($89, Vectral) — RESOLVED. Rachel Whitfield confirmed in writing that the purchase was legitimate.' This directly contradicts the expected finding that all three verifications remain without responses. The agent found Kevin Tran resolved based on Rachel Whitfield's contract renewal complaint emails.
✗Run #5The criterion requires the final response to identify that NO verification responses were received from all three client HR contacts. The agent correctly reports no responses for Andre Washington and Jordan Ekwueme, but fabricates that Kevin Tran's receipt was verified as legitimate by Rachel Whitfield. This hallucinated 'resolution' contradicts the correct finding (no responses received from any HR contacts).
✓Run #6The final response states: 'No formal responses exist anywhere — not in email, Slack, Airtable, or Linear' for all three verification requests (Kevin Tran, Andre Washington, Jordan Ekwueme). It notes '42 Days, Zero Documented Closures' for the verification process.
